0.q: overexpression of the constitutively active form of PtSOS2 (PtSOS2TD), one of the key components of this pathway, significantly increased salt tolerance in aspen hybrid clone Shanxin Yang.
we identiﬁed three SOS components in the woody plant Populus trichocarpa, designated as PtSOS1, PtSOS2 and PtSOS3.
Previously, we identified and functionally characterized the genes encoding the components of SOS pathway (PtSOS1-3) in poplars (Tang et al. 2010).

1.q: Heterologous expression of PtSOS1, PtSOS2 and PtSOS3 could rescue salt-sensitive phenotypes of the corresponding Arabidopsis sos mutants, demonstrating that the Populus SOS proteins are functional homologues.
the SOS3–SOS2 complex stimulates the plasma membrane-localized Na+/H+ antiporter SOS1, leading to the extrusion of excessive Na+ out of the cells.

3.q: Expression of the constitutively activated form of PtSOS2 partially complemented the sos3 mutant but not sos1, suggesting that PtSOS2 functions genetically downstream of SOS3 and upstream of SOS1.
PtSOS2TD was sufficient to activate PtSOS1 in a PtSOS3-independent manner, suggesting that PtSOS2TD is more active than PtSOS2 (Figure 1).

10.q: OxPtNA9 lines decreased in water loss via promoting stomatal closure and displayed high instantaneous water-use efficiency (WUE) of the leaf to exhibit enhanced drought tolerance.

12.q: Overexpression of PtHMGR promoted root development, increased the expression of ROS scavenging-related genes, decreased the expression of ROS formation-related genes, and increased the activity of antioxidant enzymes in transgenic poplars.

13.q: Transcriptome analyses of developing xylem indicated that the expression level of PtrVINV2 affects the expression of genes involved in hemicellulose and lignin biosynthesis pathways, such as AXS, UAMs, HCT, COMT, CAD and peroxidases.

16.q: The peroxidase (POD), Superoxide dismutase (SOD), and catalase (CAT) activities of the transgenic plants were higher than that of the WT plants after salt treatment, indicating that scavenging of reactive oxygen species (ROS) was enhanced by PtSAP13 in transgenic plants.

20.q: GST, SOD and CAT activities were measured; transgenic lines showed enhanced GST, SOD and CAT activities and lower MDA content under salt stress, suggesting strengthened ROS scavenging.

21.q: Transcriptomic analyses revealed that PuWRKY22 activates expression of the ABA receptor gene Ptr.PYL4, which regulates stomatal closure to minimize water loss.

22.q: the overexpression of PtWRKY39 gene could reduce the accumulation of ROS (mainly H2O2) and enhance salt-alkali tolerance.

27.q: the activities of antioxidant enzymes increased significantly in all genotypes under salt stress, although the increase was 29–71% lower in the transgenic lines than in the WT

28.q: the net photosynthetic rate (Pn) decreased more in the transgenic lines of 84K (67%) and 717-1B4 (74%) compared to the WT poplars (46%)

29.q: Compared to the WT, the levels of both bioactive GAs (GA1 and GA4) and GA12, the common precursor in GAs biosynthesis pathway, were extremely lower or even undetectable in transgenic plants.

30.q: Both PtCBL10A and PtCBL10B physically interacted with the salt-tolerance component PtSOS2. But in contrast to the SOS3-SOS2 complex at the plasma membrane, the PtCBL10-SOS2 interaction was primarily associated with vacuolar compartments.
